portionGuidance: Half a cup, cooked (about 130g).
pairingAdvice: Add a vegetable stew and fish, chicken, or meat.
logicNote: Parboiled rice raises sugar a little slower than white rice, but it is still a starch. Keep to the size shown below.

name: Baguette (French bread)
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only one short piece (about 30g). Never eat it on its own.
pairingAdvice: Eat it with an egg, beans, or moi moi. Never bread on its own.
logicNote: White flour, like agege bread. It pushes your sugar up faster than most foods.

name: Cocoyam Fufu
portionGuidance: One ball the size of your fist. That is about half a cup of the cooked swallow (100g).
pairingAdvice: A green vegetable soup, like efo riro or okra, plus fish or meat.
logicNote: A soft cocoyam swallow. More of the rough part of food than eba, but still a starch, so keep to one fist-size ball (100g).

name: Sweet Potato Porridge
portionGuidance: One small bowl. That is about three-quarters of a cup of the cooked porridge (150g).
pairingAdvice: Add plenty of vegetables and fish or meat.
logicNote: Sweet potato is gentler than yam, but the porridge is still a starch. Load the vegetables and keep to the size shown below.

name: Tomato Stew (obe ata)
portionGuidance: Two to three big spoons (about half a cup).
pairingAdvice: It already has fish, chicken, or meat. Eat with half a cup of rice (130g), or a fist-size ball of swallow (100g). The swallow matters too. Oat swallow raises sugar the least, and eba, fufu, pounded yam and semovita raise it the most.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A pepper and tomato sauce with oil and body-building food. It is low in starch, so it does not raise sugar much on its own. The rice or swallow is what to watch.
healthNote: Beef and goat are red meat. Liver, kidney, shaki, and pomo are organ meat. Both are fine for your sugar but not for everyone. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, pick fish or skinless chicken instead and use less salt.

name: Ofada Sauce (Ayamase)
portionGuidance: Two to three big spoons (about half a cup).
pairingAdvice: It already has assorted meat. Eat with half a cup of ofada rice (130g).
logicNote: A green pepper sauce with plenty of palm oil and assorted meat. Little starch, but heavy with oil, so use less oil.
healthNote: This dish is cooked with a lot of palm oil. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, use less oil and go easy on the red meat.

name: Garden Egg Sauce
portionGuidance: Two to three big spoons (about half a cup).
pairingAdvice: Eat with a fist-size piece of boiled yam (100g) or two slices of plantain (80g), plus fish or meat.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Made from garden eggs, pepper, and oil. Low in starch and friendly for sugar.

name: Pepper Sauce (ata dindin)
portionGuidance: One to two big spoons (about a quarter cup).
pairingAdvice: Use with half a cup of rice, or two pieces of yam or plantain, plus fish or meat.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Fried pepper, onion, and oil. Very low in starch. The starch it goes with is what to watch.
healthNote: This dish is cooked with a lot of palm oil. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, use less oil and go easy on the red meat.

name: Cow Leg
portionGuidance: One to two pieces (about 90g).
pairingAdvice: In pepper soup or stew, with vegetables.
logicNote: Mostly skin, bone, and collagen. Low in starch and does not raise sugar.
healthNote: Beef and goat are red meat. Liver, kidney, shaki, and pomo are organ meat. Both are fine for your sugar but not for everyone. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, pick fish or skinless chicken instead and use less salt.

name: Cow Tail
portionGuidance: One to two pieces (about 90g).
pairingAdvice: In pepper soup or stew, with vegetables.
logicNote: A fatty meat with very little starch, so it does not raise sugar. Trim the heavy fat if you can.
healthNote: Beef and goat are red meat. Liver, kidney, shaki, and pomo are organ meat. Both are fine for your sugar but not for everyone. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, pick fish or skinless chicken instead and use less salt.

name: Smoked Fish
portionGuidance: One piece as wide as your palm (about 90g).
pairingAdvice: In soups, stews, and pepper sauce, with vegetables.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Dried, smoked fish with little starch and plenty of body-building food. Rinse off the extra salt.
healthNote: This food is very salty. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, use only a pinch. Too much salt harms your heart and kidneys even when your sugar is fine.

name: African Yam Bean
portionGuidance: Half a cup, cooked (about 130g).
pairingAdvice: Fish, an egg, or two slices of plantain (80g).
logicNote: A beans food with plenty of the rough part of food and body-building food, so the sugar rises slowly.

name: Baked Beans (canned)
portionGuidance: Half a cup of the tinned beans (about 130g).
pairingAdvice: Add an egg and vegetables. Eat with one thin slice of bread (30g), not more.
logicNote: Beans are friendly, but the tinned tomato sauce has added sugar, so keep to the size shown below.
healthNote: This food is very salty. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, use only a pinch. Too much salt harms your heart and kidneys even when your sugar is fine.

name: Spring Onion
portionGuidance: One handful, chopped (about 30g).
pairingAdvice: Add to stews, sauces, and salads.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A flavour vegetable with almost no sugar.

name: Celery
portionGuidance: One to two stalks.
pairingAdvice: In soups and stews, with fish, chicken, or an egg.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Mostly water and the rough part of food, with almost no sugar.

name: Parsley
portionGuidance: One handful, chopped (about 20g).
pairingAdvice: Sprinkle into sauces, salads, and soups.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A green herb with no real effect on sugar.

name: Nigerian Salad
portionGuidance: One plate of mostly vegetables (about one and a half cups).
pairingAdvice: Add a boiled egg, chicken, or fish. Go easy on the salad cream, sweet corn, and baked beans.
logicNote: Cabbage, lettuce, cucumber, and carrot are friendly. The salad cream, sweet corn, and baked beans add the sugar and fat, so go light on those.

name: Tuna Salad
portionGuidance: One bowl (about one cup).
pairingAdvice: Cucumber, lettuce, and tomato. Go easy on the mayonnaise.
logicNote: Tuna and vegetables are low in starch and high in body-building food. The mayonnaise is the only thing to keep small.

name: Fruit Salad
portionGuidance: Half a cup of mixed fruit (about 80g).
pairingAdvice: About 10 nuts, or a small cup of plain yogurt (150g). No sugar or cream.
logicNote: A bowl of mixed fruit puts a lot of natural sugar together, so keep to the size shown below and add no sugar.

name: Sesame Seed (beniseed)
portionGuidance: One tablespoon (about 10g).
pairingAdvice: Sprinkle on salads, vegetables, and soups.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Seeds with healthy fat and the rough part of food, and very little sugar.

name: Mixed Nuts
portionGuidance: About 10 nuts (30g).
pairingAdvice: Eat as a snack, or add to salads and plain yogurt.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Assorted nuts give healthy fat, body-building food, and the rough part of food, with little sugar. Choose the kind with no salt and no sugar.

name: Peanut Butter (no added sugar)
portionGuidance: One tablespoon (about 15g).
pairingAdvice: On one thin slice of bread (30g). Do not add jam.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Ground groundnuts give good fat and body-building food. Look at the packet and choose the kind with no added sugar.

name: Curry Powder
portionGuidance: A pinch. Add it to your cooking for taste.
pairingAdvice: Use in rice, stews, and sauces.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A spice blend for flavour, with no effect on sugar.

name: Thyme
portionGuidance: A pinch. Add it to your cooking for taste.
pairingAdvice: Use in rice, stews, soups, and meat.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A herb for flavour, with no effect on sugar.

name: Chocolate Bar
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only two small squares.
pairingAdvice: Better to snack on about 10 nuts.
logicNote: Milk chocolate is mostly sugar and fat, and raises sugar fast.

name: Peanut Candy (groundnut brittle)
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only one small piece (about 30g).
pairingAdvice: Better to snack on plain groundnuts, about 10.
logicNote: Groundnuts set in melted sugar. The groundnut is fine but the sugar is not.

name: Baba Dudu (caramel toffee)
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only one small piece.
pairingAdvice: Better to have a small piece of fruit instead.
logicNote: A hard toffee made from melted sugar. It raises sugar fast.

name: Chocolate Spread
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only a thin scrape on one slice of bread.
pairingAdvice: Better to use one tablespoon of peanut butter with no added sugar.
logicNote: Mostly sugar and oil. It raises sugar fast.

name: Jam / Marmalade
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only a thin scrape on one slice of bread.
pairingAdvice: Better to top your bread with an egg or avocado.
logicNote: Fruit boiled with a lot of sugar. The rough part is gone and it raises sugar fast.

name: Flavoured Milk (chocolate / strawberry)
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. Choose plain milk with no sugar instead.
pairingAdvice: Nothing makes a sweet milk drink safe. Drink plain milk or water.
logicNote: Chocolate and strawberry milk carry a lot of added sugar in liquid form, which pushes your sugar up fast.

name: Tuwo Dawa (sorghum swallow)
portionGuidance: One ball the size of your fist. That is about half a cup of the cooked swallow (100g).
pairingAdvice: A green vegetable soup like miyan kuka or okra, plus fish or meat.
logicNote: A sorghum swallow. Whole grain, but still a starch, so keep to one fist-size ball (100g).

name: Chickpeas (boiled)
portionGuidance: Half a cup, cooked (about 130g).
pairingAdvice: In stews and salads, with vegetables.
logicNote: A beans food with the rough part of food and body-building food, so the sugar rises slowly.

name: Lentils
portionGuidance: Half a cup, cooked (about 130g).
pairingAdvice: In stews and soups, with vegetables.
logicNote: A beans food that raises sugar slowly, full of the rough part of food, and body-building. Friendly for sugar.

name: Sausage (processed)
portionGuidance: One to two sausages (about 90g).
pairingAdvice: With eggs and vegetables, not bread.
logicNote: Low in starch but processed, salty, and high in fat. Keep it occasional.
healthNote: This food is very salty. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, use only a pinch. Too much salt harms your heart and kidneys even when your sugar is fine.

name: Alkaki
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only one small piece.
pairingAdvice: Better to snack on about 10 nuts.
logicNote: Fried wheat dough soaked in sugar syrup. It raises sugar fast.
healthNote: This food is fried or heavy with oil. If you have high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or kidney problems, keep to the size shown below and use less oil. Boiled or grilled is better.

name: Radish
portionGuidance: One handful, sliced (about 100g).
pairingAdvice: In salads, or added to stews.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A crunchy root with almost no sugar.

name: Turnip
portionGuidance: Half a cup, cooked (about 80g).
pairingAdvice: In soups and stews.
logicNote: A low-sugar root vegetable with the rough part of food.

name: Bean Sprouts
portionGuidance: One cup (about 100g).
pairingAdvice: In salads, or added to stews, with fish, chicken, or an egg.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Crunchy sprouts, very low in sugar.

name: Seeds (pumpkin, sunflower, flax, chia)
portionGuidance: One tablespoon (about 15g).
pairingAdvice: Sprinkle on salads, yogurt, and oats.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: Seeds give healthy fat, the rough part of food, and a little body-building food, with almost no sugar.

name: Dried Fruit (mango, pineapple, banana)
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. If you do have it, only one tablespoon (about 15g).
pairingAdvice: Better to eat the fresh fruit instead.
logicNote: Drying takes out the water and packs the sugar tight, so even one handful pushes your sugar up fast. Fresh fruit is better.

name: Canned Fruit (in syrup)
portionGuidance: Best to skip this. Choose fresh fruit instead.
pairingAdvice: Better to eat fresh fruit, or fruit canned in water, not syrup.
logicNote: Fruit canned in syrup sits in added sugar, which pushes your sugar up fast. Fresh fruit is better.

name: Potato Salad
portionGuidance: Half a cup of the made salad (about 130g).
pairingAdvice: Add a boiled egg and vegetables. Go easy on the mayonnaise.
logicNote: Boiled potato with mayonnaise. The potato is a starch, so keep to the size shown below.

name: Mustard
portionGuidance: One to two teaspoons.
pairingAdvice: With meat, fish, or eggs.
frequency: You can eat this every day.
logicNote: A sharp yellow sauce with almost no sugar. Some kinds have honey in them, so do not buy those.
